candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture supply a non-circular, operational criterion for when a self-model is genuinely 'causally necessary' for regulation, rather than merely correlated with or epiphenomenal to control? This is the key demarcation problem: without a precise test, the view risks either over-attributing subjectivity to many control systems or collapsing into a relabeling of sophisticated feedback.

Why should self-referential, valenced regulation yield subjective experience at all, rather than only increasingly effective self-maintenance behavior? The conjecture redescribes the functional conditions under which experience appears, but the central explanatory tension remains whether 'information for itself' explains first-person presence or only its behavioral/computational role.

What is the principled threshold or scaling law for degrees of subjectivity across bacteria, animals, and artificial agents? If subjectivity is graded and substrate-independent, the open problem is to identify which architectural features—recursion depth, integration, temporal persistence, global valuation, embodiment, counterfactual modeling—matter most, and how they combine without arbitrariness.
